triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value exceeds policy limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed by policy for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to meet the LTV requirement.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum LTV for Tier A is 90%. The calculated LTV for this proposal is 91.11%, which exceeds the limit.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds policy limit
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowed by policy for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or adjusting the term to lower the monthly payment and meet the DTI requirement.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy states that the maximum DTI for Tier A is 45%. The calculated DTI for this proposal is 45.71%, which is above the threshold.

content.reasoning: The proposal was analyzed against the underwriting policy. The applicant's credit score qualifies for Tier A, which allows for a maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) of 90% and a Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio of 45%. However, the calculated LTV is 91.11%, exceeding the policy limit. Additionally, the DTI ratio is 45.71%, which also exceeds the maximum allowed for Tier A. These findings indicate that the proposal does not fully comply with the underwriting policy.
